headline: Agent infrastructure consolidated this quarter as Google Cloud pushed the Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol across its data and database stack.
body: The quarter's center of gravity was production agent tooling: Google Cloud detailed ADK and MCP integrations spanning BigQuery, Spanner hybrid search, AI-native databases, agent registries, and Looker's governed conversational analytics, alongside the launch of Gemini 3 with expanded context and multimodal tool use. Evaluation moved toward production, with Meta Superintelligence Labs presenting continuous pipelines for drift, hallucination detection, and multi-step agentic workflows, while a separate argument held that coding agents shift engineering value to system-level design. Rotation movers stayed in lagging technique territory (multi-provider support, AI-assisted development, the PIV Loop, cache hit rate, encrypted reasoning, ranking, and trajectory data), and weight perturbation weakened. New entrants clustered in lower-level methods such as primitive-action decomposition, optimization bounds, optical flow, RAFT, 360 stereo, the Discrete Hartley Transform, and Green's functions.
